pi_items.source: Standard Windows path for Docker user Documents folder. Consistent with Windows conventions.
VitalDB Python API: loaded case 6 and confirmed all three physiological parameters are available in the dataset. Case 6 contains 67 total tracks from multiple medical devices.

Medical visualization convention. Green for heart rate is standard; red for blood pressure indicates critical parameter; blue/cyan for SpO2 is standard. Colors follow established clinical monitoring display conventions.

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB (Vital Signs DataBase) - Real, publicly available dataset
pi_items.source: Web verification: VitalDB is a real open dataset from Seoul National University Hospital containing 6,388 surgical cases. Published in Nature Scientific Data (2022). Accessible via physionet.org and vitaldb.net. Case 6 is a real case with 67 physiological tracks.
HTTP verification: URL https://vitaldb.net/getvr.php?type=msi&ver=1.16.6 returns valid MSI installer (7.2 MB). Version 1.16.6 added Servo-u ventilator protocol support.
task.json id field. Follows benchmark naming convention (task_name@version).
task.json version field. Follows semantic versioning.
task.json env_id field. References the Windows environment with VitalRecorder.

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB case 6 is a real surgical case from the public VitalDB dataset (6,388 cases from Seoul National University Hospital). The case contains 67 physiological tracks including the three required parameters: Heart Rate (Solar8000/HR), Systolic BP (Solar8000/NIBP_SBP), and SpO2 (Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2). The output path C:\Users\Docker\Documents\publication_figure.png is a valid Windows path. The color scheme (Green/Red/Blue) follows standard medical visualization conventions. VitalRecorder version 1.16.6 is a real, downloadable version from vitaldb.net. However, CRITICAL ISSUE: The task references setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 scripts in the pre_task and post_task hooks, but these files do not exist in the task directory, making the task non-executable as configured.
